name: Toasted Coconut Layer Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F.
2. Generously spray three 9-inch cake pans with cooking spray and lightly flour them.
3. Spread coconut on an ungreased jelly-roll pan and toast in the oven until it begins to brown slightly. Stir often and set aside.
4. In a medium bowl, beat egg whites until stiff but not dry. Set aside.
5. In a large mixing bowl, beat together butter, sugar, vanilla, and coconut-flavored extract. Add egg yolks one at a time and beat well after each.
6. Combine flour and baking soda. Add to the creamed mixture alternately with buttermilk. Fold in egg whites and 1 cup toasted coconut. Divide batter evenly among prepared pans.
7. Bake for 25 minutes or until a toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean. Remove layers from pans and cool on a rack.
8. Frosting: Combine cream cheese, butter, and confectioners' sugar. Beat until smooth. Stir in the remaining 1 cup toasted coconut.
